query: Coffin spring vs. Slow Maxillary Expansion

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- History and Physical -- Skeletal anterior crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: Skeletal anterior crossbite is characterized by molar and canine Class III relation in centric occlusion and centric relation. An edge-to-edge incisor relation cannot be obtained in a centric relation. The malocclusion's etiology and the affected teeth' inclination should be evaluated. The upper arch expansion is more likely stable if the teeth to be moved are initially tilted palatally. The appliances used for expansion are a Coffin spring, Quad helix appliance, surgically assisted rapid maxillary expansion, and Ni-Ti palatal expander. The patient has a concave profile, a retrusive upper lip, a predominant chin, and a negative ANB angle.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: The coffin spring is an omega-shaped wire appliance that corrects crossbite in young, developing dentition. The expansion produced is slow and bilaterally symmetrical.

retrieved_docs.title: Pediatric Obstructive Sleep Apnea -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Although oral appliances (OAs) have been shown to decrease AHI in adults, there have not been enough studies on pediatric populations to know their efficacies or which patients might benefit from these. [9] Changing OAs to accommodate a child's growth would also require frequent refittings. Rapid maxillary expansion, however, is a potential option for prepubescent patients and may be especially useful for those with high arched palates and non-obese patients with residual OSA after A&T. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Le Fort Osteotomy -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: LeFort I osteotomy is used to reposition the dentition-bearing maxilla and is indicated for the following conditions: Maxillomandibular deformities Maxillary hypoplasia or mandibular hyperplasia Vertical maxillary excess Angle’s class II and class III malocclusions Midline discrepancies or asymmetries Apertognathia or open bite Absolute transverse arch discrepancy* Dual or multiplanar occlusion* Severe maxillary atrophy, in conjunction with bone grafting Obstructive sleep apnea Access for skull base tumors Facilitation of reduction of nonreducible fractures [6] [7] [8] *May require segmental or multipiece LeFort osteotomy, which is not covered in this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: There are several methods to treat malocclusion. Palatal expanders are traditionally used before braces treatment to treat a narrow palate, crowding, or crossbite. Rapid palatal expansion (RPE) works by placing a force on the two maxillary bones to split the mid-palatal suture. Palatal expanders are most useful during adolescence, as the maxillary bones become fused during puberty. The expander contains a screw turned daily or as directed by the patient's orthodontist. Every turn places pressure on the maxillary bone, allowing for less than 1 mm of expansion. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: The rapid maxillary expansion involves a hyrax screw type of appliance capable of splitting the mid-palatine suture and bringing about skeletal changes in a short interval. The RME screw can be incorporated into 2 types of appliances, the banded RME and bonded RME.

retrieved_docs.title: Upper Airway Resistance Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Oral appliances offer an effective alternative for patients with UARS who are intolerant of CPAP or decline surgical intervention (see Image . Custom Oral Appliance for Upper Airway Resistance Syndrome). [22] These devices advance the mandible and soft tissue anteriorly, increasing the airway space posterior to the tongue. Long-term use, such as for 1.5 years, has been associated with reductions in stress-related symptoms, as measured by the Inventory of Stress Symptoms. [23] Additional oral appliance options include tongue-retaining devices, soft palate lifters, and tongue stabilizers.

retrieved_docs.title: Sleep Study -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Evaluation of response to positive airway pressure (PAP), oral appliance, or positional therapy

retrieved_docs.title: Obstructive Sleep Apnea -- Etiology -- Anatomical Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Micrognathia and retrognathia Facial elongation Mandibular hypoplasia Adenoid and tonsillar hypertrophy Inferior displacement of the hyoid

retrieved_docs.title: Sleep Study -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Evaluation before upper airway surgery for snoring or OSA

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: NiTi Expanders: These nickel-titanium wire shapes can be attached to a lingual sheath welded to molar bands cemented to the maxillary first permanent molars. Various sizes are available and must be selected depending on the amount of expansion desired and the pretreatment width of the palate.

retrieved_docs.title: Apnea in Children -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: hypertrophy), stridor suggests upper airway obstruction and wheeze lower airway obstruction (asthma). Characteristic adenoid facies (underdeveloped thin nostrils, short upper lip, prominent upper teeth, crowded teeth, narrow upper alveolus, high-arched palate, hypoplastic maxilla) with mouth breathing, nasal speech, and periorbital swelling may be present in 15% to 20% of children with OSA.

retrieved_docs.title: Craniofacial Distraction Osteogenesis -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Midface Hypoplasia : Midface hypoplasia can present as a result of several craniofacial syndromes (Treacher Collins, Cohen syndrome, etc.) and in patients with isolated cleft lip or palate. Patients will often have visible malar retrusion, class 3 malocclusion, and a degree of exorbitism due to isolated retrodisplacement of the inferior orbital rim. Similar to mandibular hypoplasia, patients may present with airway obstruction or severe sleep apnea. Distraction osteogenesis has proven a useful tool in correcting these abnormalities. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Trinucleotide Repeat Disorders -- Treatment / Management -- Head and neck manifestations
retrieved_docs.content: Frequent otitis media and sinusitis in approximately 50% of affected children require antibiotics, polyethylene tubes, or both. Approximately 30% to 50% of affected individuals need ophthalmological help for strabismus, myopia, or hyperopia. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Pediatric Obstructive Sleep Apnea -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Gastroesophageal reflux - When reflux occurs at night, it can cause a brief pause in breathing that may be mistaken for OSA. The two are not, however, mutually exclusive and reflux can increase the risk of OSA by worsening adenotonsillar hypertrophy.

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Implants -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Growth patterns and predictions are important to consider when evaluating facial implants. Female individuals start and stop growing on average sooner than male individuals. [16] "Growth cessation" has many different definitions. The Craniofacial Growth Consortium Study compared different definitions of this term. Growth cessation was defined in 1 of 2 ways: growth less than 0.1 mm per year or growth rate less than 10% of the peak growth velocity. Serial cephalograms help determine growth cessation. Linear distances of series cephalograms are used to compare the growth rate. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 93

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2901
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation is recognized as a specific perinatal condition that is classified as تقييد النمو الجنيني, indicating fetal growth restriction, and also as ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}, denoting a birth at the highest degree of prematurity with an unspecified weight. This dual classification underscores the clinical importance of identifying both the limited fetal growth and the extreme prematurity associated with these presentations for appropriate medical management and follow‑up.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 151
retrieved_docs.content: (unspecified condition originating in the perinatal period), situating fetal growth restriction within a larger, undefined perinatal condition category. Together, these relationships illustrate how gestational duration, low birth weight, fetal growth restriction, and associated perinatal disorders are organized within medical classification systems, aiding clinicians in diagnosing and managing newborns with short gestation and related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2900
retrieved_docs.content: CONDITIONS OF FETAL GROWTH AND MALNUTRITION represent a collective group of perinatal problems that encompass short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation. Short gestation, defined as delivery before full term, often results in low birth weight because the infant has had insufficient time for normal growth. Low birth weight, in turn, frequently reflects fetal growth retardation, a condition in which the fetus does not achieve its genetically expected size. These three entities are closely interrelated, each contributing to the broader spectrum of fetal growth and malnutrition disorders, and together they increase the risk of adverse neonatal outcomes. Effective maternal care that optimizes nutrition and prevents premature delivery is essential for reducing the incidence of these interconnected conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2146
retrieved_docs.content: The larynx serves as an indirect procedure site for other non‑or therapeutic procedures on the respiratory system, indicating that certain respiratory interventions that are not directly therapeutic to the larynx can nonetheless involve the larynx indirectly. This relationship highlights the importance of recognizing the larynx’s involvement when planning and performing non‑therapeutic respiratory procedures, as indirect effects on the larynx may impact airway function and patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.content: and to the neurosurgical and spinal explorations. Additional Excision applications include therapeutic procedures involving the nose, mouth and pharynx and are related to plastic procedures on the nose. Together, Exploration provides an umbrella framework for targeted surgical investigations, while Excision represents the core operative technique that enables tissue removal, obstruction relief, diagnostic assessment and neural decompression, thereby optimizing patient outcomes across multiple specialties.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1843
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation; low birth weight; and fetal growth retardation are classified under the term تقييد النمو الجنيني, which denotes fetal growth restriction. This condition is understood to be part of a broader, unspecified disorder that originates in the perinatal period, referred to as حالة غير محددة نشأت في الفترة المحيطة بالولادة. Recognizing that تقييد النمو الجنيني falls within this larger perinatal context helps clinicians assess and manage infants presenting with these growth‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1981
retrieved_docs.content: Procedures on Nose are medical interventions that address nasal disorders (اِضطِراب أنفي). Within this category, control of epistaxis is identified as a specific type of Procedure on Nose, indicating that managing nosebleeds is a subset of nasal procedural care. This relationship highlights that interventions aimed at controlling epistaxis are directly linked to the broader management of nasal disorders, emphasizing the role of targeted procedural treatment in the evaluation and care of patients with nasal pathology.

retrieved_docs.content: Dilation of genitourinary system is classified as a dilation procedure.
